title: Bounded-RSS long-running interpreter loops via arena scope reclamation (Noesis BUG #6 / PRIORITY 1)
goal: Noesis field report (BUGS-2026-07-04 #6, Selene night-build note PRIORITY 1): a long-lived `eshkol-run -r` process that repeatedly read-line's a growing file and appends into an accumulating structure balloons to 9-24GB RSS and gets OOM-killed, forcing a bounded-lifetime restart-every-~80s hack on a production memory-weaver daemon. Fix at the architectural root (Eshkol is deliberately zero-GC / OALR arena-based) -- no bolted-on tracing GC -- using the existing arena-scope and with-region machinery, and make that machinery actually deliver bounded RSS in a hot loop instead of just documenting a workaround.
root_cause: Three independent, additive bugs, discovered in this order while chasing the reported 24GB OOM down to a repeatable repro: (1) MEMORY: every `read-line` call reserved a fixed 1024-byte arena string unconditionally (never freed across iterations of a persistent interpreter process), and `memory-store-append!`-shaped loops that drop their per-pass accumulator leaked the same way -- both landed in the single ever-growing REPL-persistent global arena, which has no scope boundary inside a tight loop. (2) TCO: the intended fix -- wrap each loop iteration's transient work in `(with-region ...)` so it is reclaimed via region_pop -- could not actually be used in a hot loop, because `TailCallCodegen::areAllSelfCallsInTailPosition` (lib/backend/tail_call_codegen.cpp), the analysis gating whether a named-let loop gets the branch-based TCO transform, had a dedicated `ESHKOL_IF_OP` case that is DEAD CODE: the parser always lowers `if` to `ESHKOL_CALL_OP` with a synthetic `call_op.func` named "if" (lib/frontend/parser.cpp, explicitly commented 'Store the if operation as a call to builtin if'). The sibling analysis used for `define`-level tail recursion (`findTailCalls` in llvm_codegen.cpp) already special-cases this parser quirk; named-let's analysis had drifted out of sync with it and never did. Net effect: virtually EVERY `(let loop (...) (if cond (loop ...) ...))` -- the single most idiomatic Scheme loop shape -- was silently classified as non-tail-recursive and fell back to genuine unbounded C-stack recursion, independent of any memory concern, capping any such loop at roughly 100k-500k iterations (crash: SIGBUS, misreported as 'stack overflow (recursion too deep)') regardless of how much RAM is free. Verified in isolation: `(define (f x) (if (< x N) (f (+ x 1)) x))` runs 3,000,000 iterations instantly (proper TCO); the textually-identical named-let form crashes around 100k-500k. (3) Once (2) is fixed and `with-region` actually executes as a real per-iteration branch-based loop, two more leaks surfaced in with-region's own codegen: (3a) `codegenWithRegion` created its `region_result_slot`/`escaped_result_slot` allocas at the CURRENT (in-loop) insertion point instead of the function's entry block; LLVM `alloca` re-adjusts the stack pointer every time control re-enters that block, so a with-region used anywhere inside a branch-based loop leaked native stack every iteration, again capping iteration count via a spurious 'stack overflow' well under 1M. (3b) `region_create` (lib/core/runtime_regions.cpp) allocated the `eshkol_region_t` control struct itself from whatever arena was currently active (the persistent global arena, for a with-region at loop top level) rather than from a lifetime matching its own create/destroy pairing, leaking ~48-64 bytes into the persistent arena on every with-region activation -- small, but enough to turn 1M+ iterations into visibly-linear (not flat) growth.
fix: (1) lib/backend/tail_call_codegen.cpp: added the same 'if is CALL_OP with func-name if' special case to `allSelfCallsInTailPosition` that `findTailCalls` already has, so named-let TCO eligibility analysis matches reality -- an `if`-guarded self-tail-call now correctly gets the branch-based TCO loop transform instead of silently falling back to real recursion. This is the highest-leverage fix: it applies to every named-let loop in the language, not just this bug's specific shape. (2) lib/backend/llvm_codegen.cpp `codegenWithRegion`: hoisted the two per-region allocas to the enclosing function's entry block (the same saveIP/SetInsertPoint(entry, entry.begin())/restoreIP pattern already used elsewhere in this file, e.g. for variadic-closure argument packing), so they execute once per function activation instead of once per loop iteration. (3) lib/core/runtime_regions.cpp `region_create`/`region_destroy`: switched the `eshkol_region_t` control struct (and its optional name copy) from arena allocation to plain malloc/free -- its lifetime is fully deterministic (created in region_create, destroyed exactly once in region_destroy, the only caller graph), so it does not belong in a scope-outliving arena at all; this gives with-region a strictly O(1) steady-state footprint regardless of iteration count. (4) lib/backend/string_io_codegen.cpp `readLine`: read into a fixed 1024-byte scratch buffer that is ALSO entry-block-hoisted (reused across every read-line call in the enclosing function/loop, zero allocation for the scratch itself), then allocate the final Eshkol string sized to the ACTUAL line length (not the 1024-byte capacity) and copy it out -- cuts per-call arena churn from a fixed 1024 bytes to the real line length, so even the transient per-iteration footprint inside a with-region is right-sized. None of this bolts on a tracing GC: it is entirely existing OALR arena/region machinery, working as originally intended, with the bugs that were silently defeating it fixed at the root.
acceptance: Root cause reproduced BEFORE the fix: unbounded-RSS repro scaled from 249MB (N=1000 passes) to 14.2GB (N=64000 passes) -- confirmed linear/unbounded growth on unmodified master
Same repro shape, AFTER the fix, wrapped in (with-region ...): flat ~239MB peak RSS at N=1,000,000 passes and ~92MB at N=2,000,000 passes (15M+ read-line calls) -- NOT growing with iteration count

Independent TCO-only regression confirmed and fixed: `(let loop ((x 0)) (if (< x 3000000) (loop (+ x 1)) x))` completes instantly post-fix; pre-fix it crashed (SIGBUS / spurious stack-overflow) between 100k-500k iterations with 0% memory pressure
tests/memory/long_loop_rss_test.esk: 300k passes x 50 lines/pass, PASS under both -r and AOT
scripts/run_rss_bounded_test.sh: N=100k vs N=1,000,000 (10x scale) gives peak-RSS ratio ~1.00x (flat) -- gate PASSES
SICP smoke: 88/88 (tests/sicp, both -r and AOT) -- no regression from the named-let TCO fix, the single highest-blast-radius change in this PR
Full local suite (scripts/run_all_tests.sh): clean, no new failures vs pre-change baseline

ASAN build (build-asan, ESHKOL_ENABLE_ASAN=ON): tests/memory/long_loop_rss_test.esk + tests/memory/region_test.esk + SICP smoke clean under -fsanitize=address (validates the region_create malloc/free lifetime change has no UAF/double-free)

esh_0214b_automatic_scoping.status: DONE (second commit on the same branch/PR)
esh_0214b_automatic_scoping.summary: Tier (a) -- escape-analysis-driven AUTOMATIC per-iteration arena scope reclamation for named-let TCO loops -- is now implemented; no (with-region ...) annotation needed. Design: (i) RUNTIME (lib/core/runtime_arena_core.cpp + arena_memory.h): arena_commit_scope (drop the innermost scope record WITHOUT rewinding -- keep memory, keep the scope stack balanced), arena_top_scope_contains (is ptr inside the innermost scope's allocation span? blocks are head-inserted, so span = newer blocks + [mark, used) in the mark block), and eshkol_arena_iter_scope_end(arena, vals, n) which POPS the scope when no out-flowing tagged value points into the span and COMMITS otherwise. (ii) CODEGEN (lib/backend/llvm_codegen.cpp): codegenNamedLet pushes an arena scope at the top of the tco_loop header block when the analysis approves; every TCO back edge (codegenTailCallFromContext) releases the scope over the freshly-evaluated loop-argument values BEFORE storing them to the param allocas, and the single loop-exit return releases it over the result value -- pushes and releases are strictly LIFO even across nested auto-scoped loops. (iii) STATIC ANALYSIS (namedLetIterScopeSafe / iterScopeSafeExpr): conservative walk that only enables the mechanism when the body cannot leak an iteration-allocated value through any channel OTHER than the dynamically-checked loop args / result: rejects set! and any callee whose name ends in '!', an explicit blacklist (read, string->symbol, eval, apply, call/cc, dynamic-wind, error/raise, delay/force, parallel-*, spawn, ...), unknown callees, and unhandled special forms; accepts a whitelist of allocation-escape-free first-order builtins, higher-order builtins (map/filter/fold/...) whose function argument is an inline lambda / local loop / known define, locally nested named lets, and user-defined functions analyzed recursively through function_body_ast (memoized, cycle-inductive). Anything unprovable silently disables the feature (loop behaves exactly as before). ESHKOL_NO_ITER_SCOPE=1 disables globally. (iv) The dynamic per-value check makes bignum-overflow promotion, rationals from /, and pre-loop heap values carried through args (ports!) all safe: a pre-loop pointer is outside the span so reclamation still happens; an in-span pointer flips that edge to commit (exactly the pre-feature behavior).
esh_0214b_automatic_scoping.measurements: tests/memory/long_loop_rss_auto_test.esk (the weaver shape, ZERO annotations): flat 226MB -> 225MB peak RSS from N=100k to N=1,000,000 passes under -r (identical plateau to the explicit with-region variant; the ~220MB floor is the JIT compile itself); 28.7MB peak RSS at 300k passes under AOT. Correctness: tests/memory/auto_iter_scope_test.esk 11/11 under -r, AOT, ASAN, and ESHKOL_ARENA_POISON=1 (0xCB poison-on-pop would crash any missed escape).
esh_0214b_automatic_scoping.gates_rerun: SICP 88/88; AD oracle 56/56; memory 10/10 (incl. 2 new); lists 129/129; features + stdlib 100%; v1.2 edge cases 95/95 (2 module-skips); repl 21/21; tco 7/7; runtime_arena_core_test (extended with commit/contains/iter-scope-end pop+commit cases) PASS under regular AND ASAN builds; scripts/run_rss_bounded_test.sh now gates BOTH the with-region and the automatic variant (ALL PASS).
esh_0214b_automatic_scoping.note: A freshly compiled stdlib.o now references eshkol_arena_iter_scope_end, so eshkol-repl/eshkol-server binaries must be rebuilt together with stdlib (the symbol is ADD_SYMBOL-registered in lib/repl/repl_jit.cpp); a stale REPL binary + fresh stdlib cache surfaces as 'JIT session error: Symbols not found: [ _eshkol_arena_iter_scope_end ]'.

remaining_work: do-loops: codegenDo has its own lowering separate from codegenNamedLet; extend the same push/iter-scope-end wiring to its loop form (named-let covers the reported production shape).

Self-tail-recursive DEFINEs (define-level TCO at llvm_codegen.cpp:10604) could get the same automatic scoping; their exit paths are less centralized than named-let's single return, so left for a follow-up (tco_ctx.iter_scope is explicitly cleared there).
Analysis extensions when needed: letrec-bound local lambdas as callees, case/match forms, AD operators in loop bodies (all currently conservative-disable).
read-line's fixed 1024-byte line-length cap (pre-existing, unrelated to this bug) is unchanged -- lines longer than 1023 bytes still silently split across multiple read-line calls.

The same 'if is CALL_OP' dead-code pattern should be audited for any OTHER AST analysis pass that special-cases ESHKOL_IF_OP directly (grep found `isOperationInTailPosition` in the same file has an identical dead ESHKOL_IF_OP case, but it has no callers outside its own file today, so it was left as-is rather than risk an unused-code change; flagged for cleanup).
